title: triglochin muelleri
intro: Triglochin muelleri, also known as Mueller's sedge or New Zealand sedge, is a type of flowering plant native to Australia, specifically in the eastern and southern regions. It belongs to the family Juncaginaceae and is characterized by its long, slender stems and small white flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. Triglochin muelleri can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used to support immune function and reduce inflammation.
